name: Sitjar Reservoir Loop from Onda
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse del Sitjar – Circular desde Onda
description: Cycle a moderate 16.9-mile (27.2 km) route around the beautiful Sitjar Reservoir from Onda, enjoying scenic views and paved roads.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Onda offers diverse routes through the natural gateway to the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, characterized by extensive cork oak, holm oak, and pine forests. The terrain includes challenging climbs and winding paths, with the Sonella River and Sichar reservoir providing scenic backdrops. Cyclists can navigate through varied landscapes, from orange and olive groves to unpaved horticultural paths and mountain passes. Best touring cycling routes around Onda The most popular touring cycling route is Sitjar Reservoir Loop from Onda, a 16.9 miles (27.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 46 minutes to complete, offering views of the Sitjar reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Benitandús Reservoir – St. Josep Fountain loop from Onda, a difficult 27.4 miles (44.1 km) path that traverses varied terrain around reservoirs. Local touring cyclists also love the Fuente de Castro – Onda Castle loop from Onda, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail leading through historical areas and featuring Onda Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Onda is defined by the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, river valleys, and extensive orange and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Onda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Onda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Onda?
A: Touring cycling around Onda offers a rich variety of terrain. You'll find routes winding through extensive cork oak, holm oak, and pine forests within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. The landscape also features orange and olive groves, unpaved horticultural paths, and significant elevation gains with challenging climbs like the Puerto de Eslida. The Sonella River and Sichar reservoir provide scenic backdrops for many rides.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Onda?
A: Yes, Onda offers options for various skill levels. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are 3 easy touring cycling routes available. These paths are generally less demanding, allowing beginners to enjoy the beautiful landscapes at a comfortable pace.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I see while cycling near Onda?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, known for its dense forests and mountain passes offering spectacular panoramic views. Routes often follow the Sonella River or lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For breathtaking vistas, consider routes that ascend to peaks like Puntal de l'Aljub.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural landmarks accessible by touring bike in Onda?
A: Absolutely. Onda's rich history is reflected in its landmarks. You can explore the "Castle of 300 Towers," also known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1073196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers unique panoramic views. The historic center, with its Arab-influenced urban layout, is also accessible. Other points of interest include the Prison Tower, Algepsar Cave, and Almudín Square. Some routes might also pass by charming mountain villages like Aín.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Onda?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Onda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936823553/sitjar-reservoir-loop-from-onda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitjar Reservoir Loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route offering scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Onda?
A: The touring cycling routes around Onda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the extensive forests of the Sierra de Espadán to the serene river valleys and picturesque orange and olive groves. The variety of routes, catering to different ability levels, is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, several routes in the Onda area feature reservoirs and lakes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38287293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benitandús Reservoir – St. Josep Fountain loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses varied terrain around multiple reservoirs. You can also find routes that offer magnificent views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Onda?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Onda offers 54 difficult touring routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3962391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of les Penyes Altes – Onda Castle loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural beauty with historical elements?
A: Certainly. The region around Onda is perfect for combining natural exploration with historical discovery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31929228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Castro – Onda Castle loop from Onda&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, leading you through varied landscapes while featuring historical areas and the prominent Onda Castle.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in total around Onda?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes available around Onda, offering a wide range of options for exploring the region's diverse landscapes and attractions.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, Onda is known as the natural gateway to the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, and many touring cycling routes venture into this protected area. You can experience incredible landscapes of high environmental value, characterized by cork oaks, holm oaks, and pine forests. Additionally, the Onda - El Salvador Route leads to the El Salvador Natural Park, a pleasant pine forest with picnic areas.
tips: A very quiet and picturesque place a few kilometers from the city of Onda. You can go around it on secondary roads in good condition and at all times you will have very nice views of the reservoir. It also allows you to visit the town of RIbesables.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Tour around the Sitjar Reservoir?
faqs.answer: This cycling tour is designed to be completed in approximately 1 hour and 47 minutes. This duration is an estimate for touring cyclists and can vary based on your pace and any stops you make to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the cycling route around the Sitjar Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring bicycles. You'll be cycling on secondary roads that are generally in good condition, offering a smooth experience around the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: While the route is categorized as moderate and requires good fitness, it is also described as suitable for all skill levels. Its mostly paved surface and circular nature make it accessible. Families should consider the 27.2 km distance and 345 meters of elevation gain to ensure it matches their capabilities.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Tour around the Sitjar Reservoir?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy beautiful views of the Sitjar Reservoir itself, known for its unique beauty and tranquility. The route also passes by the Sitjar Dam. There's a recreational area by the reservoir with tables and benches, and you'll have the opportunity to visit the charming town of Ribesalbes.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Tour around the Sitjar Reservoir - Circular from Onda?
faqs.answer: The nearby town of Onda serves as an excellent starting point and offers convenient facilities. Onda is equipped with bicycle parking and charging stations for electric bikes, making it a good place to leave your vehicle and begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Tour around the Sitjar Reservoir - Circular from Onda dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not specifically mention whether dogs are permitted on this particular cycling route. Generally, on public roads and paths, dogs are allowed but should be kept on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations or signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The area around the Sitjar Reservoir generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. To avoid extreme heat, spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and lush scenery. Winter can also be suitable on milder days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Tour around the Sitjar Reservoir. The route utilizes public roads and paths, making it freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several local paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as Camí de la Pedrissa, Camí Vell de l'Alcora, and Camí de la Baronia, offering potential for extended exploration if desired.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way or nearby?
faqs.answer: The town of Onda, where the tour typically starts and ends, offers various gastronomic attractions. Along the route, you might find options in Ribesalbes. There's also a recreational area by the reservoir where you can stop for a picnic.

faqs.question: What kind of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route is primarily on mostly paved surfaces and secondary roads in good condition, a touring bicycle is ideal. Road bikes would also be suitable, though mountain bikes might be overkill for the main route, unless you plan to explore unpaved detours in the wider region.
